author: śrīla raghunāthadāsa gosvāmī
verses:
harir dṛṣṭvā goṣṭhe mukura⋅gatam ātmānam atulaṁ
sva⋅mādhuryaṁ rādhā⋅priyatara⋅sakhīvāptum abhitaḥ
aho gauḍe jātaḥ prabhur apara⋅gauraika⋅tanu⋅bhāk
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

purī⋅devasyāntaḥ praṇaya⋅madhunā snāna⋅madhuro
muhur govindodyad⋅viśada⋅paricaryārcita⋅padaḥ
svarūpasya prāṇārbuda⋅kamala⋅nīrājita⋅mukhaḥ
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

dadhānaḥ kaupīnaṁ tad⋅upari bahir⋅vastram aruṇaṁ
prakāṇḍo hemādri dyutibhir abhitaḥ sevita⋅tanuḥ
mudā gāyann uccair nija⋅madhura⋅nāmāvalim asau
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

anāvedyāṁ pūrvair api muni⋅gaṇair bhakti⋅nipuṇaiḥ
śruter gūḍhāṁ premojvala⋅rasa⋅phalāṁ bhakti⋅latikām
kṛpālus tāṁ gauḍe prabhur ati⋅kṛpābhiḥ prakaṭayan
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

nijatve gauḍīyān jagati parigṛhya prabhur imān
hare⋅kṛṣṇety evaṁ gaṇana⋅vidhinā kīrtayata bhoḥ
iti prāyāṁ śikṣāṁ janaka iva tebhyaḥ paridiśan
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

puraḥ paśyan nīlācala⋅patim uru⋅prema⋅nivahaiḥ
kṣaran netrāmbhobhiḥ snapita⋅nija⋅dīrghojvala⋅tanuḥ
sadā tiṣṭhan deśe praṇayi⋅garuḍa⋅stambha⋅carame
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

mudā dantair daṣṭvā dyuti⋅vijita⋅bandhūkam adharaṁ
karaṁ kṛtvā vāmaṁ kaṭi⋅nihitam anyaṁ parilasan
samutthāpya premṇāgaṇita⋅pulako nṛtya⋅kutukī
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

sarit⋅tīrārāme viraha⋅vidhuro gokula⋅vidhor
nadīm anyāṁ kurvan nayana⋅jala⋅dhārā⋅vitatibhiḥ
muhur mūrchāṁ gacchan mṛtakam iva viśvaṁ viracayan
śacī⋅sūnuḥ kiṁ me nayana⋅śaraṇīṁ jāsyati punaḥ

śacī⋅sūnor asyāṣṭakam idam abhīṣṭaṁ viracayat
sadā dainyodrekād ati⋅viśada⋅buddhiḥ paṭhati jaḥ
prakāmaṁ caitanyaḥ prabhur ati⋅kṛpāveśa⋅vivaśaḥ
pṛthu premāmbhodhau prathita⋅rasade majayati tama
en_translation:
Lord Hari, seeing His own incomparable self reflected in a mirror in Vraja and desiring to taste His own sweetness completely—just as Rādhā or Her dearmost companions do—took another golden form and was born in the land of Bengal. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

He is sweet like a bath in the honey of love within Advaita Ācārya's heart. His feet are repeatedly worshipped by Govinda Dāsa's excellent service. His face is offered ārati by Svarūpa Dāmodara's millions of life-breaths. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Wearing a loincloth and over it an outer saffron cloth, His grand body, like a golden mountain, is completely served by its own effulgence. Joyfully and loudly singing His own sweet Holy Names, will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

That which was unknown even to previous sages—the creeper of devotion, hidden in the Vedas and bearing the fruit of pure, brilliant ecstatic love—the merciful Lord manifested that with great mercy in Bengal. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

The Lord accepted the devotees from Bengal as His own. Instructing them like a father, He said: "Chant the Names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa according to a counting method, O devotees!" Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Seeing the Lord of Purī in front of Him with great streams of love, His tall and effulgent body is bathed by the flowing tears from His eyes. He always stands at the Garuḍa pillar in the temple. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Joyfully biting His lower lip with His teeth and placing His left hand on His hip, He shines beautifully. With countless ecstatic shivers and eager to dance, He rises up in love. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

Overwhelmed by separation from the Moon of Gokula on the banks of a river, He creates another river with the incessant flow of His tears. Repeatedly falling into unconsciousness, He makes the entire world appear like a corpse. Will the Son of Mother Śacī ever again come before my eyes?

This eight-verse prayer of the Son of Mother Śacī bestows all desires. He who recites this with a very clear mind due to increasing humility—Lord Caitanya, being overwhelmed by intense mercy, will immerse him in the great ocean of love, which is famous for bestowing divine nectar.
